executiveSummary.text: SFM R2 go-live is at high risk. A flawed data assumption for the leak resolution design (capital/O&M split inverted) has invalidated the proposed future-state approach, requiring rework of solution design and the OpenGrid–PowerPlan integration. Combined with a pre-existing 9-week delay in R2 user stories, an R2 go-live slip past Aug 17 is likely. Build completion gate is May 15 — a decision point for go-live feasibility.

SFM R1 SIT is RED and projecting a 2-week delay. QA environment experienced recurring downtime since Mar 23 (suspected network/cloud misconfiguration). 23 test cases remain blocked; CGI hotfix targeting Apr 17. R1 go-live (Jun 22) is currently unchanged but risk is increasing with each testing delay.

GIS Migrations and MLS Replacement successfully went live on April 13. Hypercare is underway through May 1–8. Fred/Shenandoah MLS site go-live deferred — SMUFL data validation errors must be resolved first. TTO Gate 3 artifacts remain pending across both projects.

Integration Stabilization is carrying active scope and cost overrun risk. GIS/Corrivo hypercare introduced new scope items; PeopleSoft SIT is only 33% complete (14 of 42) with UAT starting Apr 20. Integration budget variance is tracking at +46% CY outlook. A PCR is required — trigger date Apr 22.

Program-level resource and financial controls require immediate action. Technical resource constraints remain HIGH/HIGH across SFM, GIS, and IAM. Resource forecasting is incomplete across the program (Risk 78931 — trigger Apr 22). Budget tracker variances vs. the General Ledger are unresolved across GIS/MLS, Integration Stabilization, and SFM.

topRisks.description: Invalidated Data Assumption — Leak Design Rework. Capital/O&M split was inverted in design assumption; future-state leak resolution must be redesigned. Impacts OpenGrid–PowerPlan integration. R2 go-live delay highly likely.
topRisks.mitigation: Finalize edge cases by 04/20; build complete by 05/15 to determine go-live impact; CGI to confirm additional build releases

topRisks.description: Technical Resource Constraints — SFM & IAM. Capacity constraints across key WGL technical leadership. Luisa (SFM Technical Lead) overextended; IAM coverage gaps. ~$155K budget impact from contingency.
topRisks.mitigation: Onboarding Pat + 1 additional resource for SFM technical support; IAM additional resource support being identified

topRisks.description: Vendor Accountability — CGI Delivery Risk. SFM go-live milestones at risk due to delivery setbacks, incremental scope/rework during build, and inconsistent resource availability.
topRisks.mitigation: Escalated through exec governance; formalized patch validation timelines; weekly WGL Program Leadership check-ins with CGI

topRisks.description: QA Environment Intermittent Downtime. Recurring outages since Mar 23 disrupted SIT/UAT — 2-week delay projected. Network VNET change applied Apr 11; root cause still TBD.
topRisks.mitigation: Monitor VNET fix effectiveness; review network architecture with CGI + WGL cloud architects. Can close if no further timeouts.

topRisks.description: Integration Scope Changes & Cost Overruns. New scope (Corrivo hypercare changes), delayed scope (Phase 5 EAI, WMIS Integration), and unplanned design effort driving budget overage. CY outlook +46% vs. budget.
topRisks.mitigation: Define LOE and schedule; create PCR; review with UOX Program Leadership; PMO Change Board approval. Apr 17 update: initial LOE and schedule created for Phase 8/9 hypercare and scope changes. Confirming dates with team by 04/24.

topRisks.description: Program-Level Resource Forecasting Gaps. Incomplete resource forecasting across all projects; resources incorrectly allocated in some cases. Resulting in reactive ad-hoc requests and delivery risk.
topRisks.mitigation: Reconcile forecasting vs. actuals across all projects; consolidate into OnePlan (rollout late April)

topRisks.description: Budget Tracker / General Ledger Variances. GIS/MLS, Integration Stabilization, and SFM budget trackers have large EAC variances vs. GL. PCRs or fund reallocations may be required.
topRisks.mitigation: WGL Finance + Digital PMO reconciling differences; PMs verifying EAC accuracy for PCR use

topRisks.description: Field Data Location Change Breaks Existing Reports. Field-collected data moved to forms data tables (not WR attributes), impacting RM37 and external agency reporting. R2 only.
topRisks.mitigation: CGI mapping of impacted WR attributes provided; AMS team performing schema-level impact analysis for RM37

topRisks.description: Frederick/Shenandoah Sites Deferred from Go-Live. SMUFL data upload errors prevented these sites from going live Apr 13. Sites continuing on paper until SMUFL is validated.
topRisks.mitigation: Data parsing target 04/08 completed; CHA to test data flow; site go-live to be rescheduled post-validation

items.notes: Only required for R2 — targeted Apr 18
Due TODAY Apr 17. Catch-up resource onboarded.

issueHealth.narrative: Issue volume is increasing week over week, driven primarily by SFM complexity — three active risks/issues against one project alone. Two new risks were raised this week at the program level: resource forecasting gaps (78931) and field data location changes impacting R2 reports (68866), both with trigger dates of Apr 22–24.

resourceRisks: SFM Technical Lead (Luisa Eulin) is overextended with concentrated dependency creating continuity and delivery risk across SFM. Mitigation in progress: onboarding Pat plus one additional resource with net budget impact of ~$155K from contingency. GIS single-threaded resources (Hardeep and Pulkit) remain a Hypercare risk through May 8 — coverage plan not attainable per Apr 10 confirmation. Program-wide resource forecasting is incomplete across all projects (Risk 78931), generating reactive ad-hoc requests and creating delivery risk in real time — OnePlan consolidation targeted for late April is the resolution path. Only 2 of 4 TCS resources confirmed for SFM cutover support, onboarding week of May 1. Q3–Q4 2026 SFM demand plan not yet finalized.

dependencies.risk: Leak design rework requires PowerPlan AMS to assess LOE. Until resolved (target Apr 20), R2 build schedule, budget, and go-live cannot be confirmed.
SuccessFactors integration go-live is tied to SFM go-live date. Any further SFM R1 slip cascades to this integration workstream.
HyperCare scope changes (new fields, schema changes) are adding unplanned integration work and cost overruns to Integration Stabilization.
Prolonged R2 delay will push start date for SFM Consolidation and impact the Q4 2026 / Q1 2027 demand pipeline.
PeopleSoft SIT at 33% as of this week; UAT starting Apr 20. PCR for scope/cost must be approved before program can plan to closure.
GIS J-TIMP Model dependency on GIS Migrations data cleanliness should be formally revalidated now that GIS has gone live.

changesThisWeek.text: GIS Migrations and MLS Replacement successfully went live on April 13. Hypercare commenced. 800+ users trained across 60+ sessions.
SFM R1 SIT now projecting 2-week delay — 23 test cases remain blocked. CGI hotfix committed for Apr 17.
GIS/Corrivo HyperCare has introduced new scope items per Issue 79832. LOE to be defined by EOD Apr 15; reviewed at UOX Change Board Apr 16.
Risk 78931 raised: Program-level resource forecasting gaps identified as a structural issue across all projects — trigger date Apr 22.
Risk 68866 raised: Field-collected data location change will break existing RM37 reports for SFM R2 — trigger Apr 24.
SEMCO MissDig PCR#3 approved by PMO Change Board — out for signature. Go-live moved Apr 30 → May 15 (+15 days).
MLS Replacement EAC now tracking at +18% over approved budget ($2.637M vs. $2.238M approved).
SFM QA environment downtime risk downgraded RED → AMBER. Network VNET change applied Apr 11 appears to be resolving recurring outages.

actions.text: SFM R2 Go-Live Decision Gate — confirm by May 15 whether Aug 17 go-live is viable. If leak design rework not resolved and user stories not finalized by Apr 20, assess formal schedule re-baseline for R2.
Integration Stabilization PCR — Troy Millett to present LOE, revised schedule, and PCR to UOX Program Leadership and PMO Change Board. Risk 78744 trigger date Apr 22.
Confirm Resource Plan for SFM Cutover and Hypercare. Only 2 of 4 TCS resources confirmed. Escalate any unresolvable gaps to program sponsor.
Resolve Resource Forecasting Gaps via OnePlan. PLT to reconcile forecasted vs. actual hours across all projects ahead of OnePlan rollout late April.
Gate 3 Artifact Catch-Up Plan — GIS and MLS TTO Checklist due today. Both projects need confirmed artifact completion timeline to achieve Gate 3 Exit by May 22.
MLS Budget Variance PCR Assessment. EAC tracking at +18% over budget ($398K variance). Finance and PMO to determine whether PCR is required or within contingency authority.
